events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose fault: splice outside box, in-service, industrial electrical terminations & splices. NEC 110.14 covers electrical connections - splices must be in approved enclosures/junction boxes, accessible, proper termination, torque, etc. Splice outside box is non-compliant, likely code violation, shock/arc/fire hazard, especially in-service. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? In-service heavy industrial, exposed splice could cause arc flash, electrocution, fire. Probably critical if energized. But maybe high. Need decide. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, maybe degraded/failed? Splice outside box is installed-defective and non-compliant. Could include failed if conductor integrity compromised. But likely installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete 100 (in-service). Value 0. Confidence high 0.95. Findings: splice made outside approved enclosure; exposed conductors; violation of NEC 110.14; shock/arc flash/fire hazard. Actions: de-energize circuit, remove splice, install proper junction box/enclosure, re-splice per code, torque, inspect/test, re-energize. Rationale. References: NEC 110.14, maybe 300.15 (boxes for splices). But required reference material NEC 110.14. We can include NEC 110.14 and NEC 300.15. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.
